name: Architecture View
description: VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel ("Architecture") that renders the architecture wiki as a searchable list of component cards. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui ArchPanel React component and answers its list/upsert/remove/openCard RPC calls against the repo's .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json cards — the SAME store the bundled arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) reads and writes, so cards Claude authors via seed-arch/arch_upsert and cards edited in the panel are one and the same files. Clicking a card opens its <slug>.json file in the normal editor (openCard RPC → showTextDocument); there is no in-webview graph, card-detail, or editor form. Originally ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane; the force-directed ArchGraph and the in-webview CardEditor form were later removed.
guidelines: Arch cards are JSON files under .code-workbench/.arch/<slug>.json — the same store the arch MCP server (arch-server.mjs) uses; never invent a parallel store. archHost.ts reads/writes them with an atomic tmp+rename, a per-file write lock, and a safe-slug + path-traversal guard, so a panel edit and a concurrent MCP write can't corrupt a card.

The panel UI is the shared ui ArchPanel (search + list) rendered via a React webview; do not hand-write panel markup. Pass hideHeaderTitle — the WebviewView chrome already shows the 'Architecture' pane name.

Cards are edited by opening their <slug>.json file in the normal editor, NOT by an in-webview form. Selecting a list row calls the openCard RPC (archCardPath() → openTextDocument → showTextDocument, preview:false). The '+' button upserts a blank 'New Component' card then opens its file.

archHost.ts declares its own ArchCard interface (structurally identical to the ui ArchCard). Host src/ code must NOT import the type from the JSX @code-workbench/ui barrel: the extension's src tsconfig has no jsx set and excludes webview/, so importing the barrel pulls .tsx into a non-jsx program and fails the build. Mirror this exactly the way DeadCodeItem is duplicated between mcp-core and ui.
Expose the card file path via archHost.archCardPath() (wraps the safe-slug/path-traversal guard); the openCard RPC uses it. Do not build the path ad-hoc in archView.ts.

Push live updates with a vscode.FileSystemWatcher on the arch dir (RelativePattern '*.json') that posts an 'arch-changed' event, so cards written by seed-arch / the arch MCP appear without a manual refresh. Dispose the watcher on view dispose and re-point it on workspace-folder change via onRepoChanged().

The webview entry must stay in lockstep across three places: esbuild.mjs entryPoints ('webview/arch'), the WebviewEntry union in reactWebview.ts ('arch'), and ArchViewProvider.viewId / the package.json view id ('codeWorkbench.arch') + view/title refresh command.
anti_patterns: Do not implement arch-card CRUD anywhere but archHost.ts, and keep its on-disk format byte-compatible with arch-server.mjs (same fields, atomic write) so the panel and the MCP server never clobber each other's files.
Do not import @code-workbench/ui (a JSX package) from extension host src/ code — only the webview/*.tsx entries may; host code uses the local ArchCard interface in archHost.ts.
Do not re-add an in-webview card-editor form or a graph/nodes view — cards are edited as .json files in the normal editor and the panel is list-only.
Do not leak the FileSystemWatcher: dispose it on view dispose and before re-pointing it, or repo switches accumulate watchers.
decisions: The Architecture panel was ported from the removed Electron app's ArchWikiPane into the shared ui package so it renders identically; the Electron useArchWiki/window.api hook was replaced by an injected ArchApi adapter wired over the webview postMessage RPC bridge (webviewRpc.ts / bridge.ts).
The force-directed ArchGraph nodes view was removed; the panel is now a search + list only.

The in-webview CardEditor form was removed (commit f04702d, feat: add openCard). Cards are now edited by opening their <slug>.json file in the normal editor via the openCard RPC; the old openFile RPC and the read-only detail pane were dropped with it.
Live refresh uses a VS Code FileSystemWatcher instead of the Electron app's chokidar + coalesce watcher; event debouncing is delegated to VS Code's watcher.
